title: Apparatus for minimizing closing force of a door
abstract: An apparatus is provided for minimizing closing force of a door. The apparatus comprises a housing adapted to be disposed in the hinged edge of the door. A piston is slidably mounted in the bore. A button having an inner end and an outer end is disposed in the bore with the inner end of the button engaging the piston for urging the button toward a first extended position where the button extends from the edge of the door. The button is slidable in the bore relative to the housing for reciprocal movement between the extended position and a second position where the button is depressed into the bore against the force of the piston. The button is disposed so that the outer end of the button engages the door frame for slowing movement of the door in a closing direction as the button is moved into the bore to the depressed second position.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. An apparatus for minimizing closing force of a door pivotally mounted at one edge in a door frame at least partially defining an opening, the door sized and shaped to cover the opening defined by the door frame in a closed position of the door, the door closing force minimization apparatus comprising: a housing including an inner surface defining a longitudinal bore with a closed end and an open end; a piston slidably mounted in the bore, the piston including a hollow cylinder closed at both a proximal end and a distal end, a piston head moveable within said cylinder, a piston rod connected to the piston head and slidingly extending through the distal end of the cylinder for engaging the inner surface of the housing at the closed end of the bore, and a compression spring disposed on the piston rod between the distal end of the cylinder and the piston head; and a button having an inner end and an outer end, the button disposed in the bore with the inner end of the button engaging the proximal end piston for urging the button toward a first extended position where the outer end of the button extends from the open end of the housing, the button slidable in the bore relative to the housing for reciprocal movement between the first extended position and a second position where the button is depressed into the bore against the force of the piston, wherein the housing is adapted to be disposed in the edge of the door so that the outer end of the button engages the door frame for slowing movement of the door panel in a closing direction as the button is moved into the bore in a direction from the first position to the depressed second position. 2. The door closing force minimization apparatus as recited in claim 1, further comprising a radial flange extending outwardly from an outer surface of the housing proximate the open end of the bore. 3. The door closing force minimization apparatus as recited in claim 2, further comprising a stop plate defining an opening, wherein the stop plate is configured to be mounted to the flange on the housing such that the opening is aligned with the bore for slidingly receiving the button for movement of the button relative to the stop plate. 4. The door closing force minimization apparatus as recited in claim 3, further comprising a radial flange extending outwardly from an outer surface of the button proximate the inner of the button, wherein diameter of the flange is larger than the opening in the stop plate for engaging the stop plate at the first position of the button. 5. The door closing force minimization apparatus as recited in claim 3, wherein in the depressed second position the outer end of the button is contiguous with the stop plate. 6. The door closing force minimization apparatus as recited in claim 1, further comprising a second spring disposed in the cylinder between the piston head and the closed proximal end of the cylinder. 7. The door closing force minimization apparatus as recited in claim 1, wherein the piston is a pneumatic device and the cylinder accommodates a pneumatic medium as an operating fluid to cause fluid damping. 8. The door closing force minimization apparatus as recited in claim 7, wherein the pneumatic medium is air. 9. The door closing force minimization apparatus as recited in claim 7, wherein the pneumatic medium is hydraulic fluid. 10. A door comprising: 

 a door frame adapted for at least partially defining an opening in the wall; a door panel sized and shaped to cover the opening defined by the door frame in a closed position of the door, the door panel pivotally mounted at one edge in the door frame; and an apparatus for minimizing closing force of the door panel, the door closing force minimization apparatus comprising a housing including an inner surface defining a longitudinal bore with a closed end and an open end; a piston slidably mounted in the bore, the piston including a hollow cylinder closed at both a proximal end and a distal end, a piston head moveable within said cylinder, a piston rod connected to the piston head and slidingly extending through the distal end of the cylinder for engaging the inner surface of the housing closed end of the bore, a compression spring disposed on the piston rod between the cylinder and the piston head; and a button having an inner end and an outer end, the button disposed in the bore with the inner end of the button engaging the proximal end piston for urging the button toward a first extended position where the outer end of the button extends from the open end of the housing, the button slidable in the bore relative to the housing for reciprocal movement between the first extended position and a second position where the button is depressed into the bore against the force of the piston, wherein the housing is disposed in the edge of the door so that the outer end of the button engages the door frame for slowing movement of the door panel in a closing direction as the button is moved into the bore in a direction from the first position to the depressed second position for minimizing closing force of the door panel. 11. The door as recited in claim 10, further comprising a radial flange extending outwardly from an outer surface of the housing proximate the open end of the bore. 12. The door as recited in claim 11, further comprising a stop plate defining an opening, wherein the stop plate is configured to be mounted to the flange on the housing such that the opening is aligned with the bore for slidingly receiving the button for movement of the button relative to the stop plate. 13. The door as recited in claim 12, further comprising a radial flange extending outwardly from an outer surface of the button proximate the inner of the button, wherein diameter of the flange is larger than the opening in the stop plate for setting the first position of the button relative to the housing. 14. The door as recited in claim 12, wherein in the depressed second position the outer end of the button is contiguous with the stop plate. 15. The door as recited in claim 10, further comprising a second spring disposed in the cylinder between the piston head and the closed proximal end of the cylinder. 16. The door as recited in claim 10, wherein the piston is a pneumatic device and the cylinder accommodates a pneumatic medium as an operating fluid to cause fluid damping. 17. The door as recited in claim 16, wherein the pneumatic medium is air. 18. The door as recited in claim 16, wherein the pneumatic medium is hydraulic fluid. 19. The door as recited in claim 10, wherein the door closing force minimization apparatus comprises a plurality of door closing force minimization apparatuses disposed in the edge of the door panel.
